Normal: The scan shows healthy kidney tissue with no visible anomalies, cysts, or tumors.
Tumor: A solid mass was detected. This requires immediate clinical evaluation by an oncologist or urologist for biopsy and treatment planning.
Cyst: A fluid-filled sac was detected. Kidney cysts are often benign and common as people age, but should be monitored by a doctor.
Stone: A hard deposit of minerals and salts (kidney stone) was detected. Treatment depends on the size and location.
